overview.brief_summary: A sophisticated cyber attack targeting transportation systems, involving multiple stages of reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) Windows Registry Application Log Process File Network Traffic Command DNS Record
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method Artificial Intelligence Reg.exe Utility Process Creation (Mechanism) Code Injection Windows Registry Key Creation (Mechanism) Application Log Content (Mechanism) Debugger Evasion Command Execution (Mechanism) Mimikatz Tool Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about transportation systems using artificial intelligence to analyze open-source intelligence.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker identifies vulnerabilities in transportation system networks by monitoring process activity using reg.exe utility.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-70: Try Common or Default Usernames and Passwords
stage.description: The attacker develops a StrongPity-like malware to target Android users by creating a process.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker compromises user's device through malicious app download via drive-by compromise.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker injects malicious code into the compromised device.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker schedules tasks to maintain persistent access by modifying the Windows registry.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker gains elevated privileges on the compromised device by exploiting a public-facing application.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-702: Exploiting Incorrect Chaining or Granularity of Hardware Debug Components
stage.description: The attacker avoids detection by anti-virus software through debugger evasion.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.technique_name: T1558: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker accesses sensitive credentials by stealing or forging Kerberos tickets.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC-226: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: The attacker dumps stored credentials from the compromised device using mimikatz tool.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker moves laterally within the network using remote services.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data from compromised devices by accessing files.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates stolen data using alternative protocols.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts transportation system operations by causing a denial-of-service.

Scenario.summary: This complex cyber attack targets transportation systems, compromising user devices, gaining privileged access, collecting sensitive data, and disrupting operations.
